steps.title: Spot the past time reference
steps.content: The phrase "in 1868" establishes a specific completed point in the past. The sentence needs a finite main verb — a verb that can stand alone as a predicate.

steps.title: Apply the finite verb rule
steps.content:
A main clause requires a finite (conjugated) verb. Only simple past tense works for a specific past event with "in 1868."

A) to begin — infinitive; not a finite verb and cannot function as a main predicate. Wrong.
B) began — simple past finite verb; correctly anchors the main clause to the past event in 1868. Correct.
C) beginning — present participle; non-finite, cannot serve as a main verb without an auxiliary. Wrong.
D) having begun — perfect participle; non-finite, functions as a modifier, not a main verb. Wrong.

B is correct.
